title: `quiver:fork-slice` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver fork-slice <slice> --into <newId>` that splits an oversized slice into two, carrying over a user-selected subset of `files[]` and `acceptance[]`, and updating STATUS.md accordingly.
description: When a slice grows beyond its intended scope mid-execution, the current workaround is to delete and rewrite two slice.json files by hand and update STATUS.md. This command automates the split safely: it copies the original slice directory to a new ID, prompts for which `files[]`, `acceptance[]`, and `must[]` items move to the new slice, and updates STATUS.md so both slices appear. The original slice's remaining scope is preserved.
must: Copy the source slice directory to `slices/<newId>/`
Present an interactive prompt listing `files[]`, `acceptance[]`, and `must[]` items for user selection (moved vs retained)
Update both slice.json files with the resulting split
Update STATUS.md to insert the new slice row in order
Require `--yes` to bypass prompts in scripted environments (with `--files`, `--acceptance`, `--must` lists)
Refuse to fork a slice whose `status` is `completed`
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row marked `experimental` and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Auto-splitting based on file count or size heuristics
Merging two slices back into one
acceptance: `npx create-quiver fork-slice <slice> --into <newId>` creates the new slice directory
Both slices' `files[]`, `acceptance[]`, and `must[]` cover the union of the original scope with no overlap
STATUS.md lists the new slice in the right order
Attempting to fork a completed slice exits non-zero
Non-TTY invocation without selection flags exits non-zero with a clear message
assumptions: Users prefer interactive selection over CLI-driven scripting for the first version
STATUS.md parsing/updating can reuse the machinery from `lint-spec`
Splitting mid-execution is rare enough that the command never needs to be fast
blocked_reason: Deferred: needs â‰Ą1 real case of a slice that grew too big
